creator: wblock@FreeBSD.org
text:
The patch works, and the icons are all present.  It looks very nice.

However, there are a couple of problems that prevent our use of this.

First, there are political situations where the flag for a particular area is in dispute.  If we use flags, one of the disputing groups might block our site on that alone.  Trying to use flags for non-disputed areas but text for disputed ones might still be a problem (recognizing that those areas are disputed).

Second, a single flag does not represent all of the areas where a language is spoken.  For example, English is the national language in at least seven countries that come to mind.  Which flag should be used?  The opposite side of that situation is that some countries have more than one national language, and representing them with a flag does not help the reader determine which language they are selecting.

For these reasons, we cannot use national flag icons in this way.  I apologize for the difficulty, and the delay in response.  Your effort is appreciated, and we encourage such efforts to improve the website and documentation.
